result.stdout:
trcover
Copyright (c) 2023 Ken Domino
# trcover

## Summary

Analyse a grammar for test coverage of parser rules.

## Description

Given a trgen-generated driver program, and inputs, parse
the input and find how the rules were covered in the grammar.
Generate a "cover.html" file that contains the analysis for
display in a browser.

## Usage

    trcover (<string> | <options>)*

## Details

This command is useful for analyzing the inputs used to test
a grammar.

## Examples

## Current version

0.23.43 Fixes to trgen templates.

## License

The MIT License

Copyright (c) 2025 Ken Domino

ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT,
TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM, OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE
SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE SOFTWARE.


  -d, --dll        Search for parser in dll with this specified name.
  -f, --file       Read parse tree data from file instead of stdin.
  -i, --input      Parse input string.
  -p, --parser     Location of pre-built parser (aka the trgen Generated/
                   directory)
  -q, --quiet      (Default: false) Do not output anything; only set error code.
  -t, --type       Override type of parse.
  -v, --verbose    
  -x               Read input file names from stdin.
  --version        
  --help           Display this help screen.
  --version        Display version information.
  value pos. 0
